sop.label: SOP
sop.title: Start here: customize one Vault UI.
sop.description: Use this SOP to create, register, implement, preview, check, and package a controlled Vault UI without reading the whole repository first. Most implementation work happens in one Vault folder.

modeTabs.customDescription: Default view: controlled Vault business UI inside the Vault Information shell.
modeTabs.miniAppDescription: 8888-token binding, Mini APP shell, broader creative space with stricter human review.

quickStart.title: Give your Agent one prompt, and it can generate a controlled Vault UI.
quickStart.description: Paste this prompt into your AI coding agent, replace the placeholders, and it can generate a controlled custom Vault UI from this repository.
quickStart.prompt: Please generate a custom Flap Vault UI in this repository. My folder name is <your-vault-name>, UI name is <My Vault UI>, target network is <BNB Chain mainnet | BNB Testnet>, binding mode is <factory | Vault without factory | token without factory>, factoryAddress is <0x... | none>, Vault address is <0x... | none>, Workbench/E2E test tokenAddress is <real deployed ERC20 0x... ending in 7777>, preview tokenAddress is <same as test token | another real token | optional>. Production caRestrictionMode is <none | reserved | verified>, and production restricted CAs are <none | 0x..., 0x... for Workbench/registry only, not public manifest>. Note: any match.bindings[].tokenAddresses, including tokenAddresses inside a factory binding, must be real readable ERC20 addresses ending in 7777; in factory mode they are test-token proof only, not production CA restriction. Fixed extra contract targets are <none | address + label + purpose>. If needed, write them to match.bindings[].externalContracts. Action availability is <internal-market | dex-listed | both | read-only>. If I do not explicitly request a UI style, use the scaffold default surface / NiePan-style abstract template as the only visual default; built-in examples are behavior references only. My minimal Vault ABI plus UI workflow is: <paste ABI, primary reads/writes, approve spender, native value, refetch points, empty states>. Read AGENTS.md, agent-contract.json, docs/ai-agent.md, and docs/ui-pattern-snippets.md first, then scaffold, implement, preview, run vault:check, and package.

developerEntry.title: Read the small entry set, then implement in one Vault folder.
developerEntry.description: If you are a developer or AI Agent, use /example as the compact default visual baseline. Use the real host-backed examples for runtime validation, and use the neutral reference examples only for behavior patterns.
developerEntry.realExamplesDescription: These two routes already carry real token, factory, vault, and full-host runtime defaults. Use them first when validating real local development behavior.
developerEntry.referenceExamplesDescription: These routes remain as neutral behavior fixtures for reward, phase-gating, and action-state patterns. They should not override the scaffold default / NiePan-style abstract visual baseline.

cards.title: Read first
cards.body: This is the minimum entry set. Do not expand into other folders first.
items: AGENTS.md
docs/ai-agent.md
agent-contract.json

cards.title: Implement here
cards.body: Default Vault UI owns four core source files. Mini App may also include reviewed top-level audio files. Registration is handled by script.
items: src/vaults/{folder-name}/Component.tsx
src/vaults/{folder-name}/manifest.json
src/vaults/{folder-name}/VaultABI.ts
src/vaults/{folder-name}/i18n.json

cards.title: Run in order
cards.body: Check and package scripts tell you what to fix next.
items: yarn vault:scaffold {folder-name} ...
yarn vault:register {folder-name} # if not scaffolded
yarn dev
yarn vault:check {folder-name}
yarn vault:package {folder-name}

developerEntry.doneBody: blocking is 0, registration exists, /{folder-name} previews, package succeeds, and the zip contains the script-generated flap-vault-package.json marker with runtimePackageGitHead.

miniApp.label: Mini App SOP
miniApp.title: Mini App is a fullscreen business component bound to an 8888 token.
miniApp.description: Mini App is still a Flap controlled source package: it ships the same four core Vault files, may additionally package reviewed top-level audio assets, and still runs vault:check, vault:e2e, and vault:package. The difference is that it uses the Mini APP preview shell instead of the default Vault Information shell, and routing is bound by token address. The current test-stage scope is real 8888 tokens, the no-tax path, and the U-pool direction, customized case by case through manual review/audit. Mini App allows broader external capabilities and creative expression within Flap's supported range, but publish review is stricter and more human-led; the code checker is an assistive gate, not the full approval decision.

quickStart.title: Tell the Agent the mode, 8888 token, and Mini APP preview shell up front.
quickStart.description: Mini App is not a free-form webpage container. When you paste this prompt, make token-only binding, the 8888 suffix, the current no-tax/U-pool test-stage scope, a full-height root layout, and the human-review notes for external capabilities explicit.
quickStart.prompt: Please generate a Flap Mini App source package in this repository. Folder name is <mini-app-folder>, Workbench internal UI name is <My Mini App>, flap.sh displayTitle is {"zh":"<中文名>","en":"<English Name>"}; manifest.mode must be mini-app. Target network is <BNB Testnet | BNB Chain>. Binding must use only chainId + match.bindings[].tokenAddresses. tokenAddress is <real deployed ERC20 0x... ending in 8888>; do not write factoryAddress, vaultAddresses, global tokenAddresses, restrictTokenAddresses, or caPolicy. For the current test stage, describe the requirement as a real-8888, no-tax, U-pool Mini App, and assume final publish is case-by-case manual review/audit. The Component root layout must include min-h-[100vh], min-h-screen, min-h-full, or h-full. Preview uses the Mini APP shell, factoryAddress and vaultAddress are zero addresses, and the artifact area is fullscreen. Within Flap's supported range, Mini App can carry broader creative ideas and broader external capabilities, but the checker is only an assistive code gate and publish requires stricter human review; list every external endpoint, resource, frame, external link, fixed contract target, and audio/visual asset with purpose, data source, license, risk, and fallback. If BGM or sound effects are needed, put lowercase top-level audio files directly under src/vaults/<folder> and statically import them from Component.tsx; allowed extensions are .mp3, .wav, .ogg, .m4a, and .aac. Do not use remote audio or hidden autoplay; provide visible mute/pause controls and mobile fallback. Mini App only skips the default Vault UI host risk-status tag check; every other source-package, i18n, contract-boundary, and E2E rule still applies. Read AGENTS.md, agent-contract.json, docs/ai-agent.md, and docs/ui-pattern-snippets.md first, then implement, preview, run vault:check, vault:e2e, vault:package, and vault:verify-package.

summary.title: In one line: Mini App = token-scoped 8888 artifact, not factory/Vault-scoped UI.
summary.description: When writing a Mini App, treat it as a controlled Vault UI package with stronger binding and a larger display area. It can feel like a game, campaign screen, or more creative interaction inside the artifact area. The current test-stage intake is real 8888 tokens, the no-tax path, and the U-pool direction. Within Flap's supported range, external capabilities are broader than the default Custom UI path, but review is more human-led.
items: Manifest must set `mode: "mini-app"` and bind only through `match.bindings[].tokenAddresses` using a real 8888 token.
Current test-stage Mini Apps are case-by-case 8888 no-tax / U-pool builds and must pass manual review/audit.
Local preview uses the `/mini-app`-style shell: the header shows `MINI APP`, and the artifact content area can be fullscreen.
Only the default Vault UI host risk-status tag check is skipped; all other validation, file-boundary, and package rules still apply.
External capabilities, resources, links, frames, endpoints, and fixed contract targets can be requested within the allowed range, but they must go through stricter human review.

miniApp.requirementsTitle: Mini App needs these extra creative inputs.
miniApp.requirementsDescription: These are not required for the default Vault UI. The default Vault UI stays focused on Vault business workflows; when Mini App needs a small-app-like experience, describe these capabilities early so the template can constrain them and reviewers can inspect them.

requirements.title: App name
requirements.body: `manifest.name` remains the Workbench internal UI name. Mini App also requires `manifest.displayTitle.zh` and `manifest.displayTitle.en`, for example `{ "zh": "蝴蝶农场", "en": "Butterfly Farm" }`. This display title appears on flap.sh Mini App pages and tabs; `MINI APP` is only the mode label. The default Vault UI still uses the Vault Information shell.

requirements.title: Animation
requirements.body: Mini App may request richer animation. Prefer CSS transitions/keyframes, React state, and already allowed UI/icon capabilities. Complex animation, external animation resources, or extra dependencies must document purpose and risk for human review; default Vault UI does not require animation config.

requirements.title: BGM / sound
requirements.body: If a Mini App needs BGM or sound effects, put lowercase top-level audio files directly under `src/vaults/<folder>` and import them from `Component.tsx`. Provide source, license, play timing, visible mute/pause controls, failure fallback, and mobile impact. Remote audio and hidden autoplay are not allowed; default Vault UI does not allow local audio assets.

requirements.title: Visual space
requirements.body: Mini App gets a fullscreen artifact area and may use larger visuals, but the first screen should not be meaningless whitespace or an oversized middle image. Explain where core gameplay, primary action, and state live. Large images, backgrounds, canvas, and external visual resources need review.

requirements.title: SDK usage
requirements.body: The SDK is a set of small functions and hooks, not something developers should rebuild. Requirements should name the needed `@/src/sdk` capabilities, primary reads/writes, refetch points, wallet/network states, and fallback behavior; do not create an SDK-like wrapper.

miniApp.comparisonTitle: Main Differences Between Mini App and Custom Vault UI.
miniApp.comparisonDescription: Custom UI remains the default view. Mini App is a separate mode: not an unlimited webpage container, but the same controlled source package with a different binding mode, preview shell, external-capability boundary, and review strategy.

comparisonRows.custom: Uses the default `Vault Information` preview shell. The host owns token header, breadcrumb, close control, width, and shared summary.
comparisonRows.miniApp: Uses the Mini APP preview shell. Page title and tab use `manifest.displayTitle.zh/en` according to the active language, which is shown on flap.sh Mini App pages; `MINI APP` is only the mode label.

comparisonRows.custom: Supports `chainId + factoryAddress`, `chainId + vaultAddresses[0]`, or token-scoped no-factory binding.
comparisonRows.miniApp: Token-scoped only: `chainId + match.bindings[].tokenAddresses`. Do not write factory or Vault bindings.

comparisonRows.custom: Package proof token must be a real deployed ERC20 ending in `7777` or `8888`.
comparisonRows.miniApp: Bound token must be a real deployed ERC20 and must end in `8888`.

comparisonRows.custom: Default Custom Vault UI moves through the Vault business requirement, declared bindings, and checker rules.
comparisonRows.miniApp: Test-stage Mini Apps are scoped to 8888 no-tax / U-pool builds; publish is case by case and requires manual review/audit.

comparisonRows.custom: Must read and render host Vault/TaxInfo riskLevel, with warning/danger copy when missing.
comparisonRows.miniApp: Skips the default Vault risk-status tag check only. Security, resource, contract, and i18n checks still apply.

comparisonRows.custom: Avoid external endpoints, external resources, externalFrames, arbitrary outbound links, and fixed extra contract targets by default.
comparisonRows.miniApp: Broader within Flap's supported range, but every external capability must document purpose, source, risk, and fallback for human review.

comparisonRows.custom: `vault:check`, E2E, and package proof are the main gates; human review focuses on exceptions.
comparisonRows.miniApp: The checker only assists with code issues. Final approval depends more on human review of creativity, external dependencies, interaction boundaries, and launch risk.

comparisonRows.custom: Best for controlled Vault business flows: reads, status, actions, claims, approve/write.
comparisonRows.miniApp: Creative experiences are encouraged within the allowed range, but cannot bypass Flap review, package boundaries, or safety floors.

comparisonRows.custom: Does not require animation, BGM, or sound-effect config by default; usually stays static, compact, and business-first.
comparisonRows.miniApp: May request animation, BGM, and sound effects. Audio can be packaged as reviewed top-level files under `src/vaults/<folder>`, with resources, license, controls, and fallback declared for human review.

comparisonRows.custom: Declare fixed Vault workflow reads/writes, approve, marketPhase, riskLevel, and related states.
comparisonRows.miniApp: Still uses the small `@/src/sdk` function set, but requirements must name the gameplay functions and states clearly; do not create an SDK-like wrapper.

comparisonRows.custom: Default is a 768px Vault body; use `layout: "fullscreen"` only when Flap explicitly asks for it.
comparisonRows.miniApp: The Component outer root must include `min-h-[100vh]`, `min-h-screen`, `min-h-full`, or `h-full`.

comparisonRows.custom: Runtime may include token, factory, and Vault addresses; preview matches manifest bindings conservatively.
comparisonRows.miniApp: Preview uses token address as the core context; factory/Vault runtime addresses are zero addresses.

comparisonRows.custom: Four-file source package, then check/e2e/package/verify before submitting the zip.
comparisonRows.miniApp: Same four core files plus optional reviewed top-level audio assets, and the same check/e2e/package/verify path before submitting the zip.

preview.title: Flap Farm Mini App preview mode
preview.description: This screenshot comes from the flap-farm-vault E2E preview. It shows the Mini APP shell, fullscreen artifact area, and host/manifest self-test panel.
preview.imageAlt: Flap Farm Mini App preview mode screenshot
preview.caption: The screenshot explains preview mode; it does not make this package the homepage template.

workflow: Confirm this is a Mini App, not the default custom Vault UI.
Confirm the real deployed `8888` token address and chainId; do not commit placeholder addresses.
Confirm the requirement fits the current 8888 no-tax / U-pool test-stage scope; otherwise pause for manual evaluation before implementing.
Set manifest `mode: "mini-app"` and use only token-scoped binding.
Set `manifest.displayTitle.zh` and `manifest.displayTitle.en` to the real display titles, for example `{ "zh": "蝴蝶农场", "en": "Butterfly Farm" }`; it is shown on flap.sh Mini App pages.
Add a full-height class to the Component root; do not rebuild the host navbar, wallet connect, or Mini APP shell.

If animation, BGM, sound effects, or large visual resources are needed, list source, license, controls, fallback, and mobile impact first. Audio files may be placed directly under `src/vaults/<folder>` only for Mini App mode.
Describe SDK usage: which `@/src/sdk` function or hook, read/write method, state, and error fallback the app needs.
If external capabilities are used, list the purpose, data, risk, and fallback for each endpoint, resource, frame, external link, or externalContracts target for human review.
Keep the four core files, i18n, SDK/UI import rules, and contract boundaries unchanged; Mini App audio is the only local-asset exception.
Run `yarn vault:check <folder-name>` and follow JSON code / fixHint / agent.nextActions until blocking is 0.
Run `yarn vault:e2e <folder-name>`, `yarn vault:package <folder-name>`, and `yarn vault:verify-package dist/<folder-name>.zip`.
rules: `mode` may only be `mini-app`; default custom UI must omit mode.
Mini App binding may use only `tokenAddresses`; do not write factoryAddress or vaultAddresses.
The Mini App token must end in `8888` and must be a real deployed ERC20.
The current test stage first supports the 8888 no-tax / U-pool direction; other shapes need manual evaluation before implementation and cannot ship on checker pass alone.
Mini App must provide `manifest.displayTitle.zh` and `manifest.displayTitle.en`; this is shown on flap.sh Mini App pages.
The root layout must be full-height; missing that class is blocked by `mini-app-layout/missing-full-height-root`.
Animation should prefer local CSS/React. Extra animation libraries, audio, external media, or complex visual resources require human review.

BGM/sound may be submitted as reviewed top-level audio files under `src/vaults/<folder>` in Mini App mode only; do not use hidden autoplay, and provide visible mute/pause controls plus unavailable-state fallback.
Do not treat Mini App as an arbitrary webpage container; direct wallet APIs, iframe, eval, undeclared endpoints, and undeclared fixed contract targets are still blocked.
Mini App can request broader external capabilities within the supported range, but those items require stricter human review and cannot ship on checker pass alone.
Mini App only skips the default Vault UI risk-status tag check. It does not skip source-package, E2E, manifest, i18n, or security rules.
miniApp.doneBody: manifest.mode=mini-app, token-only 8888 binding, current 8888 no-tax / U-pool test-stage scope, full-height root layout, vault:check blocking=0, E2E covers PC/iPad/H5 plus phase/wrong-network states, and package + verify-package both pass. If broader external capabilities are used, the matching human review must also be complete.

scopeItems: Build the Vault UI inside the component structure provided by this template.
Use the folder name only for the folder and preview route; vault:scaffold generates the manifest artifactId automatically.

Declare Vault UI binding targets in the manifest with chain plus either factory address or a single Vault address. If a deployment needs a token CA reference list or fixed extra contract targets, declare them only inside the relevant match.bindings[] entry.
Avoid external endpoints, external resources, and fixed extra contract targets; special cases must be declared and approved by Flap review before publish.
Keep default Vault UI folders limited to Component.tsx, manifest.json, VaultABI.ts, and i18n.json. Mini App may additionally include reviewed top-level audio files.
Register local preview routing with vault:scaffold or vault:register; do not hand-edit registration unless the script cannot parse the index file.
Do not add helpers, nested components, non-audio local assets, docs, or any other files under the Vault package.
Local preview renders with safe default fixture addresses and accepts real runtime addresses through URL params.
Submission packages keep source files; production runtime JS is built by the Flap Artifact Workbench.

agentGuide.title: AI Agent / Skill Usage
agentGuide.description: Agents should work only around the entry files, core Vault files, allowed Mini App audio assets, and check output. Do not let the Agent expand the folder structure.
inputs: folder name and binding targets: chain/factory for factory mode, or chain/Vault plus optional token for no-factory mode.
Optional per-binding token CA lists belong only in match.bindings[].tokenAddresses; when present, preview/runtime uses them as part of binding matching.
Vault address is required for the core no-factory path. Token restrictions stay as optional binding-scoped tokenAddresses.
Fixed external contract targets only when the UI must call a non-token/non-Vault/non-factory contract; provide externalContracts entries with address and label.
Vault ABI is required. Standard ERC20 ABI is already provided by @/src/sdk; custom Token ABI is needed only for special token mechanics.
Read/write methods, approve spender, UI workflow, real preview addresses, and i18n requirements; avoid oracle usage unless required, and let vault:check surface it for Flap review when unavoidable.
agentGuide.outputsTitle: Only These Files May Be Generated or Edited
workflow: Read the required docs and Skill first, then pick the closest pattern.
When no UI style is specified, use the scaffold default surface / NiePan-style abstract template as the only visual default; examples are behavior references only.
For a new Vault package, run yarn vault:scaffold {folder-name} with chain/factory for factory mode, or chain/Vault plus optional token for no-factory mode; it generates artifactId automatically.
Implement only inside the four core Vault files; Mini App may add reviewed top-level audio files. Do not add helpers, subcomponents, images, local docs, or sample data.
Run yarn vault:register {folder-name} when scaffold did not create the package, then preview /{folder-name}.
Use only @/src/sdk and @/src/ui for shared capabilities; default Vault UI may locally import only ./VaultABI, while Mini App may also statically import reviewed top-level audio files.
Use safe preview defaults for first render, then pass real runtime data through URL params such as chainId, tokenAddress, and vaultAddress.
Run yarn vault:check {folder-name} and follow agent.nextActions until blocking issues are zero, then run yarn vault:package {folder-name}.

steps.title: Install dependencies
steps.body: Use Yarn for all local commands.

steps.title: Create or edit the Vault folder
steps.body: Use /example as the compact scaffold-style default visual baseline. community-buyback-example and flapixel-example are real host-backed runtime references. dex-listed-example and action-gallery-example remain neutral fixtures for phase gating and action-pattern behavior only. For real work, create a lowercase kebab-case folder name, let scaffold generate the manifest artifactId, and edit only the four files under src/vaults/{folder-name}.

steps.title: Register local preview route
steps.body: Before opening /{folder-name}, make sure the folder is registered in the local preview module map. Scaffold does this automatically; if the four files already exist, run the register command.
items: This is only local preview wiring, not a production publish step.
The registration file is not included in the Vault source package zip.
If /{folder-name} returns 404, run yarn vault:check {folder-name} and fix preview-registration/missing-vault-module.

steps.title: Implement the Vault UI
steps.body: Keep the component previewable, checkable, and packageable.
items: Use @/src/sdk for chain, wallet, contract, oracle, i18n, notification, parsing, and transaction utilities.
Use @/src/ui for shared UI primitives.
Do not add extra files or subfolders under src/vaults/{folder-name}.
Routes render with safe preview defaults; pass real preview addresses through URL params such as chainId, tokenAddress, and vaultAddress when testing a specific Vault.
Use public aliases for shared capabilities. The only local relative import allowed is ./VaultABI; do not use ./helpers, ../VaultABI, nested component imports, local asset imports, or dynamic imports.
Reuse one UI across testnet, mainnet, or other factories when the logic is the same; the binding list only describes which deployments the shared UI is intended for.
In factory mode, Flap runtime can derive the Vault address. In the core no-factory path, use exactly one Vault address and optional binding-scoped tokenAddresses.
If the UI calls a fixed extra contract target, declare it as match.bindings[].externalContracts before running check.
Keep ABI fragments minimal.
Keep Vault component copy in src/vaults/{folder-name}/i18n.json.
Do not use direct wallet APIs, iframe, eval, script injection, remote runtime imports, undeclared endpoints/external resources, or undeclared fixed contract targets.

steps.title: Start local preview
steps.body: Run the dev server, then open the registered folder route.

steps.title: Run preflight
steps.body: You must run check. It is the pre-package validation gate; if blocking is not 0, no deliverable zip should be produced.

steps.title: Package for Artifact Workbench
steps.body: You must use the package command to create the delivery zip. It runs check again and writes the flap-vault-package.json marker, runtimePackageGitHead provenance, and file hashes; manually assembled zips without this marker or with mismatched hashes are rejected by the Flap Artifact Workbench.

rules: Use a 3-64 character lowercase kebab-case folder name for folder and route.
Preview registration is local wiring only; use yarn vault:register {folder-name} when scaffold did not register the folder.
Keep the scaffold-generated artifactId in manifest.json unique and declare the required match details.
Do not submit private keys, API keys, or local environment files.
Do not bind Vault UI by type fields.
Avoid external endpoints, external resources, or fixed extra contract targets.
Special non-oracle endpoints must be declared in the manifest and reviewed by Flap; declaration does not guarantee approval.
Undeclared endpoints, external resources, or fixed extra contract targets are rejected.
All Vault CLI failures return JSON with code, fixHint, and agent.nextActions.
You must run yarn vault:check {folder-name} before packaging and follow agent.nextActions until blocking is zero.
You must use yarn vault:package {folder-name}; Workbench accepts only script-generated zips with flap-vault-package.json, runtimePackageGitHead, and matching hashes.
The Vault package folder may contain only Component.tsx, manifest.json, VaultABI.ts, and i18n.json; no helpers, assets, nested folders, docs, sample data, symlinks, or extra files.

Manifest fields are limited to artifactId, name, match, i18n, and optional endpoints. Binding-scoped tokenAddresses, vaultAddresses, and externalContracts live only under match.bindings[]. Do not add chainIds, id, owner, version, sdkVersion, actions, oracles, media, fallback, contracts, global tokenAddresses, restrictTokenAddresses, or caPolicy.

match.bindings must contain unique targets: chainId + factoryAddress, chainId + exactly one vaultAddress, or chainId + tokenAddress. Optional tokenAddresses belong only inside each binding entry; no-factory bindings may contain multiple tokens. Duplicate addresses are rejected.

No-factory manifests must remain valid for Vault-only, token-only, and Vault+token mappings. Vault+token mappings may carry multiple tokenAddresses and should emit one binding key per token for Workbench/flap.sh mapping review.
All visible Vault component copy must live in i18n.json, and every key used by Component.tsx must exist in every locale declared by manifest.i18n.
Every Vault UI must read and visibly render the current contract risk status from host Vault/TaxInfo context, including a clear missing-risk warning when riskLevel is unavailable.

Vault source may import only approved packages and shared surfaces: react, viem, decimal.js, lucide-react, @/src/sdk, @/src/ui, and ./VaultABI. CommonJS require(), dynamic import(), unapproved dependencies, and extra SDK packages are blocked.
Do not use direct wallet APIs, eval, the Function constructor, iframe, script injection, dangerouslySetInnerHTML, or runtime remote imports.
Do not use direct browser network, media, storage, navigation, Worker, cross-context messaging, permission APIs, or browser-global aliasing/destructuring/computed access.
Direct fetch() targets must be static absolute HTTPS URLs without credentials and covered by manifest.endpoints. Host-relative, dynamic, HTTP, credentialed, or undeclared fetch targets are blocked.

Do not add third-party images, remote media URLs, data URLs, Arweave, WebSocket, or non-HTTPS resources. Token media must come from Flap-controlled runtime context such as context.tokenImageUrl; immutable Vault-specific images must use IpfsImage with a static image CID.
Component-owned links must stay on the active chain explorer through context.explorerBaseUrl or shared explorer helpers; arbitrary external navigation is blocked.

Contract reads, writes, event watches, log/filter calls, and gas estimates must stay inside the Vault/token/NFT/factory/declaration boundary and use runtime context, Vault-derived addresses, or declared externalContracts. Routers, bridges, aggregators, or unrelated app contracts are blocked.
Do not hardcode EVM addresses in Vault source. Use context.vaultAddress, context.tokenAddress, or approved runtime context values.
Keep ABI fragments minimal. Use erc20Abi or standardErc20Abi from @/src/sdk for standard ERC20 methods, and keep transaction math as bigint or Decimal instead of Number(...).
If VaultABI.ts uses human-readable ABI signatures, wrap them with parseAbi([...]) from viem; raw signature string arrays are blocked.

Write-capable Vault UIs must state whether actions run in internal-market, dex-listed, both, or read-only mode, use context.host?.marketPhase with isActionAvailableForPhase(...), and show unavailable states instead of hiding actions.
Chain-specific write flows must check sdk.wallet.isWrongNetwork and use sdk.wallet.switchChain() or render a clear switch-network state before any write.
Polling and refetch intervals must not be faster than 5 seconds unless Flap explicitly approves the need.
After yarn vault:package {folder-name}, run yarn vault:verify-package dist/{folder-name}.zip to verify marker, file list, metadata, hashes, duplicate entries, and filename/header consistency.

yarn vault:check, yarn build, yarn runtime:package, and yarn vault:package must pass the template freshness gate against npm latest @flapsdk/vault-runtime; stale versions and version-only edits are blocked.
If shared runtime surfaces changed, run yarn runtime:package and yarn runtime:verify-package to validate the local shared runtime package and runtime-contract.json.
For shared runtime, checker, preview shell, or code-base changes, run yarn ci when feasible; it covers lint, typecheck, checker selftest, package/verify, build, runtime verify, and preview smoke.

preview.phasePanelDescription: Switch the template preview host context used by context.host.marketPhase.
preview.phasePanelHint: This only changes local preview context. Production uses the Flap host to inject equivalent token phase data.
preview.phasePanelSourceRuntime: Using the real host phase now. Use the buttons below to temporarily override the local preview phase.
preview.phasePanelSourceOverride: Using a manual phase override now. Click Real to restore the live host phase.
preview.hostReadPanelDescription: Lists the preview host values that were actually read from chain or overridden locally.
preview.hostReadPanelHint: Use this block to verify whether the current preview is running in full-host, onchain fallback, or an unavailable state before validating a Vault flow.
preview.imagePanelDescription: Uses on-chain ERC20 symbol/name plus an optional host image override for preview context.
preview.imagePanelError: No usable token image override was provided.
preview.imagePanelHint: Vault components should read context.tokenImageUrl when they need token media; do not fetch private token metadata APIs from Component.tsx.
preview.tokenUnavailableDescription: This token cannot be loaded in the preview. Ask the developer to check the token CA, chain, and manifest factory/Vault binding before using this Vault UI.
